title: asplenium viviparum
intro: Asplenium viviparum, also known as the walking fern, is a type of plant that reproduces itself by producing small plantlets that grow on the mother plant's fronds. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Asplenium viviparum can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
